fields.helpText: This field contains the Qtr/Yr of the Annual Report entry.
This field contains the region number of the facility.
This field contains a Y or N if the facility is multi division.
This field contains the RPM Classification which is number 1-6 used to classify facilities by size, function, and specialty programs for resource allocation purposes.
This field contains the Complexity Level which is number 1-4 that is determined by VACO criteria concerning size and complexity and upon which grade structure is based.
This field contains Y or N if there are VA Sponsored Dietetic Internships.
This field contains Y or N if there is Affiliated AP4.
This field contains Y or N if there is Affiliated Dietetic Internship.
This field contains Y or N if there is Affiliated Cup.
This field contain Y or N if there is VA Sponsored AP4.
This field contain Y or N if there is Affiliated Dietetic Technicians.
This multiple contains the categories of the Specialized Medical Programs.
This multiple contains the categories of the Primary Delivery System.
This field contains the number of Bedside Tray Service.
This field contains the number of Cafeteria Service.
This field contains number of trays delivered to patients' table in a dining Area.
This field contains the number of Inpatients being treated.
This field contains the number of Outpatients that visited the Satellite Clinics.
This multiple contains the Locations of the Satellite Clinics and the number of Outpatients that visited them.
The Total Meals Served calculated.
This field contains the count of all personnel assigned to the Dietetic Service and includes full-time and part-time employees.
This field contains the FTEE of the Clinical Dietitians and Clinical Technicians who work with the Clinical Dietitians in patient care activities.

This field contains the FTEE of the Chief and Assistant Chief of the Dietetic Service, Chief of the Administrative Section, Chief Clinical Section, Administrative dietitians, internship, education and staff development dietitians and any QA dietitians.
This field contains the FTEE of all secretaries, clerk-typists, cost accountants and administrative technicians; it does not include the diet communications personnel.
This field contains the FTEE of all the supervisory cooks and the supervisory food service workers.
This field contains the Modified Diet Snapshot date for building the list of dates in Tabulating the total Modified Diet and calculating the average of a week for each quarter.
This field contains both the number of unscheduled hours and intermittent hours worked by measured personnel.
This multiple contains the Cost Per Diem and Cost Per Meal.
This multiple contains the Dietetic Equipment available.
This field is the total Consolidated Memorandum Report (CMR) Equipment inventory Cost.
This field contains the date the Nutritive Analysis Average for one week regular menu was taken.
This field contains the amount of Calories taken from the Nutritive Average of one week regular menu.
This field contains the percentage Carbohydrates taken from the Nutritive Analysis Average of one week regular menu.
This field contains the percentage Protein taken from the Nutritive Analysis Average of one week regular menu.
This field contains the percentage Fat taken from the Nutritive Analysis Average of one week regular menu.
This field contains the milligrams Cholesterol taken from the Nutritive Analysis Average of one week regular menu.
This field contains the milligrams Sodium taken from Nutritive Analysis Average.
This field contains the number of the Specialty Staff Certified Diabetes Educators (CDE) at your facility.
This field contains the number of Specialty Staff Certified in Nutrition Support at your facility.
This field contains the number of Specialty Staff Registered Clinical Dietetic Technicians at your facility.
This field contains the number of Specialty Staff with Clinical Privileges (not scope of practice).

fields.fieldName: DO YOU USE COOK CHILL FOODS?
fields.helpText: This field contains a Y or N on whether the Dietitians use Cook Chill Foods.

fields.setOfCodes: 1:LESS AND EQUAL 25% OF MENU ITEMS;2:GREATER THAN 25% OF MENU ITEMS;
fields.helpText: This field will be asked if the Dietitians use Cook Chill Foods. It contains a 1 for less and equal to 25% of menu items cook chilled or a 2 for greater than 25% of menu items cook chilled.
This field contains a Y or N on whether the Dietitians have Funded Nutrition Research.
This field contains the Assigned Clinical FTEE on the Funded Nutrition Research.
This field contains a Y or a N on whether the Dietitians have Unfunded Nutrition Research.
This multiple contains a list of Areas of Research under the Unfunded Nutrition Research.
This field contains the Assigned Total Clinical FTEE of the total of all the Areas of Research selected under the Unfunded Nutrition Research.

This multiple contains the numbers surveyed in regards to the five ratings, Very Good, Good, Average, Fair, and Poor, for question one of the Dietetic Survey on whether the food looked attractive, appetizing.
This multiple contains the numbers surveyed in regards to the five ratings, Very Good, Good, Average, Fair, and Poor, for question two of the Dietetic Survey on whether the food tastes good.
This multiple contains the numbers surveyed in regards to the five ratings, Very Good, Good, Average, Fair, and Poor, for question three of the Dietetic Survey on the temperature of the food.
This multiple contains the numbers surveyed in regards to the five ratings, Very Good, Good, Average, Fair, and Poor, for question four of the Dietetic Survey on the variety of foods served.

This multiple contains the numbers surveyed in regards to the five ratings, Very Good, Good, Average, Fair, and Poor, for question five of the Dietetic Survey on whether the dishes, cups, and utensils were clean.

This multiple contains the number surveyed in regards to the five ratings, Very Good, Good, Average, Fair, and Poor, for question six of the Dietetic Survey on whether the Dietetic Service staff were courteous.

This multiple contains the numbers surveyed in regards to the five ratings, Very Good, Good, Average, Fair, and Poor, for question seven of the Dietetic Survey on whether there was adequate time to eat.

This multiple contains the numbers surveyed in regards to the five ratings, Very Good, Good, Average, Fair, and Poor, for question eight of the Dietetic Survey on the diet information given by the dietitian/dietetic technician.

This multiple contains the numbers surveyed in regards to the five ratings, Very Good, Good, Average, Fair, and Poor, for question nine of the Dietetic Survey on the overall quality of the food service.

This multiple contains the numbers surveyed in regard to the five ratings, Excellent, Very Good, Good, Fair and Unacceptable for question #1 of the Dietetic Survey on whether the food looked appealing and appetizing.

This multiple contains the numbers surveyed in regard to the five ratings, Excellent, Very Good, Good, Fair and Unacceptable for question #2 of the Dietetic Survey on whether the meals include preferred foods.
This multiple contains the numbers surveyed in regard to the five ratings, Excellent, Very Good, Good, Fair and Unacceptable for question #3 of the Dietetic Survey on whether the food was hot enough.

This multiple contains the numbers surveyed in regard to the five ratings, Excellent, Very Good, Good, Fair and Unacceptable for question #4 of the Dietetic Survey on whether items such as juice, milk and desserts were cold enough.

This multiple contains the numbers surveyed in regard to the five ratings, Excellent, Very Good, Good, Fair and Unacceptable for question #5 of the Dietetic Survey on whether NF&S employees were polite.

This multiple contains the numbers surveyed in regard to the five ratings, Excellent, Very Good, Good, Fair and Unacceptable for question #6 of the Dietetic Survey on whether patient's food likes and dislikes are discussed.

This multiple contains the numbers surveyed in regard to the five ratings, Excellent, Very Good, Good, Fair and Unacceptable for question #7 of the Dietetic Survey on whether meals are served about the same time each day.

This multiple contains the numbers surveyed in regard to the five ratings, Excellent, Very Good, Good, Fair and Unacceptable for question #8 of the Dietetic Survey on whether the patient had enough time to eat.

This multiple contains the numbers surveyed in regard to the five ratings, Excellent, Very Good, Good, Fair and Unacceptable for question #9 of the Dietetic Survey on whether the patient received nutritional information  or an individual diet plan.

This multiple contains the numbers surveyed in regard to the five ratings, Excellent, Very Good, Good, Fair and Unacceptable for question #10 of the Dietetic Survey on the overall quality of the N&FS.
